progressionRules.rule: Hold the weight until all three sets hit 12 clean reps, then add 2.5 kg.

exerciseLibrary.name: Goblet squat to box
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: 3×15 at 20 kg done (15 Jul) — legs out-lasting the lungs. Next step: move to leg press (or barbell box squat) to load the legs properly, since the goblet gasses you before it challenges them. Still controlled, still box depth.

exerciseLibrary.name: Dumbbell bench press
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Stay at 22 kg until all three sets hit 12 clean — then 24 kg.

exerciseLibrary.name: Cable stack lat pulldown
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: 55 kg next session. The 50s moved well.

exerciseLibrary.name: Plate-loaded lat pulldown
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Hold 90 kg total (45 kg/side) until 3×12 is clean — hit 3×10 on 15 Jul. Then add the smallest plate per side. Log by total plate weight, labelled.

exerciseLibrary.name: Wide-grip cable row
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: LEAD with this next session — skipped 15 Jul to keep under the hour. 40 kg, own 3×12, then 42.5. Your main horizontal pull; don't let it keep dropping off the end.

exerciseLibrary.name: Cable lateral raise
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: 3×12 at 5 kg done (15 Jul). Build to 3×15 strict, or nudge to 6–7 kg only if form stays clean — no swinging. Delts grow on reps, not load.

exerciseLibrary.name: Seated cable row (close grip)
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Retest — that number looks generous next to your wide-grip row.

exerciseLibrary.name: Tricep pushdown
exerciseLibrary.coachNext: Baseline set 15 Jul: working weight 40 kg × 12 (hard). Next: one warm-up, then 3×12 at 40 kg near failure. Then climb.

cardio.note: watch logged this as Outdoor Walk — corrected to Indoor

exercises.name: Plate-loaded lat pulldown
exercises.note: 45 kg per side (plate-loaded, total plate weight); clean baseline for this machine

exercises.name: Goblet squat to box
exercises.note: cardio/breathing the limiter, not the legs

exercises.name: Tricep pushdown
exercises.note: baseline established; ramp-ups 30 kg×15, 35 kg×15, then working set 40 kg×12 (hard at end)

coachNotes.title: Session #4 — back under the bar, and it counted
coachNotes.note: Seven days off, then a full-body session that mattered. Bench 22 kg finally clean 3×12 — 24 kg next time, earned. Squat 20 kg 3×15 (PB) — goblet's near its ceiling, leg press next. Plate pulldown honest baseline at 90 kg total. Tricep pushdown baseline set at 40 kg. Kept it to the hour and skipped the row to protect consistency — right call. Row leads next session.
lines: Bench 22 kg: 3×12 clean, last rep a grind — that's owned. 24 kg is banked for next session, no argument.
Goblet squat 20 kg: 3×15, and you called it yourself — lungs quit before the legs. It's done its job. Leg press next to load the legs properly.
Plate-loaded pulldown: sorted the machine mix-up — 90 kg total (45/side), 3×10, hard. Hold there, build to 3×12.
Triceps: baseline established. 40 kg × 12 was the real working set. One warm-up then 3×12 at 40 near failure next time.
Lateral raise 5 kg 3×12 strict. Row skipped to keep the session under the hour — adherence over grinding a junk set knackered. It leads next session.
First lift in seven days. Don't let that gap open again — three days max.

coachNotes.title: Under 260 — milestone down, now get back under the bar
coachNotes.note: 259.8 lb this morning. That is the Under 260 milestone gone — from 266.0 on 29 June, real numbers only. Credit where it's due: you kept the walks going through four days flat out at work. But two honest flags: the scale's dropping fast (protect the muscle), and it's seven days since you lifted. Book a session today or tomorrow — the walking holds the deficit, the lifting holds the muscle.
lines: 259.8 lb — Under 260 is down. Next flag on the board: 5% lost at 252.7 lb, then 18 stone. Mark this one, it's real.
The walks were the win of the week — a lunch walk every day while slammed at work is exactly the adherence that keeps this going. That NEAT is why the scale moved.
But it's moving quick: 263.2 on the 9th to 259.8 today is over 3 lb in six days — hotter than our 1–2 lb/week pocket. Some of that is water, but it's the point where we start protecting muscle.

The real problem: seven days since session #3. Your rule is three. Walking is not lifting — without the resistance signal, fast weight loss starts eating muscle, not just fat. Full-body session today or tomorrow, no later.
Weigh in every morning this week. If the daily numbers confirm 3+ lb/week, we add ~250 kcal of protein-dense food to guard the muscle. One lever at a time — but the bar comes first.

coachNotes.title: Four lunch walks — the deficit's quiet workhorse
coachNotes.note: Slammed at work but got a lunch walk in every day, 11–14 July. Four outdoor walks in Doncaster, ~3.8 km each, ~500 kcal apiece — near 2,000 kcal of free deficit across the week without denting recovery. This is exactly how you hold progress through a busy stretch.
lines: Sat 11: 49 min, 3.82 km, 499 kcal, avg HR 124.
Sun 12: 48 min, 3.78 km, 483 kcal, avg HR 121.
Mon 13: 48 min, 3.76 km, 491 kcal, avg HR 138.
Tue 14: 50 min, 3.89 km, 510 kcal, avg HR 132.
Consistent around 12'50"/km, heart rate mostly Zone 2. Steps are the quiet workhorse of fat loss and you leaned on them when you couldn't get to the gym — right instinct.

coachNotes.title: Recovery day done right — treadmill + swim
coachNotes.note: No bar today, and that was the plan. 30 min treadmill (1.55 km, HR 122) then a 31 min pool swim — 720 m breaststroke, HR 122. Both bang in Zone 2, exactly the easy I asked for. ~1,000 kcal of free deficit without touching tomorrow's recovery. Lift tomorrow.
lines: Treadmill: 30 min, 1.55 km, 322 kcal, avg HR 122. Steady and honest — no ego, just movement.

Pool: 31 min, 720 m breaststroke, 36 lengths, 678 kcal, avg HR 122. That heart rate is textbook Zone 2 — you kept it easy like I asked, so it widens the deficit without stealing from tomorrow's session.
Roughly 1,000 kcal across the two, and neither adds recovery debt. This is how you bookend a rest day.
Protein floor still stands — 180 g today. Weigh in tomorrow morning, then hit the full-body work fresh.

coachNotes.title: Session #3 complete — 45 minutes, two walks
coachNotes.note: Strength done: 45 min, avg HR 132, 540 kcal. Bookended by two indoor walks — 15 min warm-up, 30 min cool-down. The cool-down logged on the watch as Outdoor; it was indoor, corrected. Fasted session — 40–50 g protein within the hour: did it land?
lines: 45:02 of strength at avg HR 132 for 540 kcal. That is a proper session — not a wander round the gym.
Warm-up: 15 min indoor walk, 969 m, HR 115. Cool-down: 30 min indoor walk, 1.55 km, HR 130. Both indoor — the watch mislabelled the second as Outdoor, now fixed.
Add it up: 926 kcal across the three pieces. Good day's work, and the walking is free deficit — keep bookending sessions like this.
You trained fasted. Protein floor is 180 g and the fasted rule is 40–50 g inside the hour. Tell me it went in.

coachNotes.title: Record cleared — real data only
coachNotes.note: Wiped eight weigh-ins that were never stood on. Two real numbers stand: 266.0 lb baseline on 29 June, 263.6 lb on 30 June (smart scale, 34.8% body fat, BMI 35.7). That 2.4 lb is mostly first-week water, not fat — don't over-read it. Weigh tomorrow and we start the real trend.
lines: The log had ten daily weigh-ins. You stood on the scale twice. The eight fakes are gone — I coach off data, so the data gets honest first.
What's real: 266.0 lb baseline (29 June), 263.6 lb (30 June) with 34.8% body fat and BMI 35.7 off the smart scale. That composition number is the one worth watching.
Two points is not a trend. I'm calling nothing on rate of loss until we've got a week of real mornings.
Weigh in tomorrow. Then every morning you can manage. The scale is data, not judgement — but it only helps if you actually stand on it.

coachNotes.title: Fasted session — PBs and one telling-off
coachNotes.note: Bench 22 kg for 12, 10, 13 — set two wobbled, set three answered. Hold 22 until 3x12 is clean. You trained fasted: 40–50 g protein within the hour. No exceptions.
lines: Goblet squat to box: 20 kg for 3×12 — that is +60% on baseline in ten days. We hold here and build to 3×15 before adding load. The tibia sets the pace, not your ego.
Bench: 22 kg for 12, 10, 13. Set two wobbled, set three answered. Keep 22 kg until all three sets hit 12 clean.
Plate pulldown: 50 kg × 12, × 12, then × 10 — the last set told the truth. Stay at 50.
You trained fasted. Fine — but 40–50 g protein within the hour. No exceptions.
Now the uncomfortable bit: six days between sessions #2 and #3. Life got busy — I know. Your rule is never more than three. Book the next one before you leave the building.

coachNotes.title: Good density, better decisions
coachNotes.note: Row moved to 40 kg mid-session — right call. Pulldown ladder 40/45/50: 50 kg is the working weight now. 49 minutes at avg HR 132 is good density.
lines: Row: 35 kg moved too fast, so you went to 40 mid-session. Right call — that is what auto-regulation looks like.
Pulldown ladder 40/45/50: 50 kg is your working weight now.
49 minutes, average HR 132. Good density for a strength session — you are not resting on your phone.
Protein was excellent this week. Keep the streak.

coachNotes.title: Baseline day
coachNotes.note: Baselines set across the board. Squat starts light on purpose — technique before bravado. Weigh in every morning: the scale is data, not judgement.
lines: We started the squat light at 12.5 kg on purpose. You are cleared to train, not cleared to be daft — lower body progresses on technique, not bravado.
Bench at 18 kg and seated row at 60 kg give us clean starting points. Everything here has room to climb quickly.
Weigh in every morning. The scale is data, not judgement.
